mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals strong aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where both HOUST and INDPRO exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 280 erodes real returns, prompting central bank tightening that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 280.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of periods where INDPRO declines by 1%+ monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real sales (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where both RSAFS and UNRATE meet thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals economic distress, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE increases by 0.5pp+ monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 50%+ of periods where GDPC1 declines by 1%+ quarterly.
